etymology.description: 中(中) (zhōng, "middle") 山(山) (shān, "mountain") 區(區) (qū, "district")
etymology.interpretation: 中(中) (zhōng, "middle") suggests centrality or being at the center, often used in names to signify the importance or central position of a place. 山(山) (shān, "mountain") refers to a mountain or a hilly area, commonly appearing in geographical names to indicate natural topography or to honor Sun Yat-sen (孫中山), a revered figure in Chinese history known as the "Father of the Nation." Therefore, 中山(中山) (Zhōngshān, "central mountain") often signifies a place named in honor of Sun Yat-sen. Finally, 區(區) (qū, "district") denotes an administrative division or area. In the context of 中山區(中山區) (Zhōngshān Qū), it indicates a district named Zhongshan.

examples.chinese: 中山区(中山區)是台北的重要商业区(是台北的重要商業區)。
examples.pinyin: Zhōngshān Qū shì Táiběi de zhòngyào shāngyè qū.
examples.english: Zhongshan District is an important commercial area in Taipei.

examples.chinese: 许多游客喜欢在中山区购物(許多遊客喜歡在中山區購物)。
examples.pinyin: Xǔduō yóukè xǐhuān zài Zhōngshān Qū gòuwù.
examples.english: Many tourists enjoy shopping in Zhongshan District.

examples.chinese: 中山区(中山區)有许多咖啡馆(有許多咖啡館)，晚上特别热闹(晚上特別熱鬧)。
examples.pinyin: Zhōngshān Qū yǒu xǔduō kāfēiguǎn, wǎnshàng tèbié rènào.
examples.english: Zhongshan District has many cafes, and it is especially lively at night.
